commit.message:
Refactor integration test CMake setup

Eliminates DRY violations and fixes infinite recursion bug in test collection.

Key changes:
- Replace broken `tenzir_collect_tests()` with CTest's built-in test enumeration
- Add `tenzir_build_test_command()` helper to centralize tenzir-test invocations
- Automatically include plugin tests from contrib/tenzir-plugins/*/test
- Add `--all-projects` flag to integration-tests target

Example usage:

tenzir_build_test_command(cmd TEST_ROOT "test/compiler")

tenzir_build_test_command(cmd TEST_ROOT "test/compiler")

non-project directories. Refresh supporting tests to cover the new
detection logic, logging paths, and empty-project output.

commit.message:
Port tests to new testing framework (#5495)

This PR is a massive one that concerns our infrastructure:

1. I removed our singleton `run.py` script, which is now a stand-alone
repository at github.com/tenzir/test.
2. I migrated all tests from `tenzir/tests/*` to `/test*`, using the new
test harness.
3. I re-implemented the runners specific to this repo in
`test/runners/*`.
4. I switch all tests to the new YAML-style frontmatter, which required
a lot of AST/IR baseline updates.
5. I split the CMake `test` target into `unit-tests` and
`integration-tests` to run those separately in CI.
6. I ported all relevant Bats tests and retired TQL1 tests
7. I removed Bats vestiges from the repo
